title: Doctrine text stops misinforming Codex sessions, everywhere the claim is live
action: Per D1/D2/D22. STEP 1 — rewrite the guardrails sentence in skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md:80 ('On Claude Code these are enforced mechanically by hooks; on Codex you must honor them yourself'). The replacement states exactly three things: (a) both runtimes ship hooks rendered from one shared catalog — .codex/hooks.json, 8 lifecycle events, onboarding-tracked at .bee/onboarding.json:51; (b) the enforcement floor is the shared helpers, identical on both runtimes; (c) whether an installed Codex CLI actually EXECUTES those hooks is unverified, so self-honoring the guardrails remains the fallback on any runtime whose hook execution is unconfirmed. STEP 2 — correct every stale corner: docs/02-architecture.md:219 ('a coherent 6-hook automation skeleton for Claude Code'), :221 ('hooks are Claude Code's second, mechanical belt') and :223 ('Any proposed seventh hook must name which of the six it replaces'); README.md:312; README.md:444 ('7 lifecycle events' — measured count is 8); README.md:479; INSTALL.md:63; docs/01-distillation.md:36; docs/05-roadmap.md:69; docs/06-runtime-integration.md:40 and :5-6 (the 'Claude Code gets a hook-driven skeleton / Codex gets the helper-enforced skeleton' framing that survives the D1 fix verbatim); docs/07-contracts.md:104 (the buildSessionPreamble payload contract, which D6 changes) and :216 (the bee-session-init hook-table row). Measured ground truth for these: `ls hooks/bee-*.mjs` = 9 hook scripts; `Object.keys(require('./.codex/hooks.json').hooks).length` = 8 lifecycle events; hooks/claude-hooks.json = 7 events. Use the right number for the right quantity — do not replace one false count with another. STEP 3 — propagate: run `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs` (renders .claude-plugin/ and .codex-plugin/) then `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply` (syncs .claude/skills, .agents/skills and merges the root AGENTS.md). This cell runs FIRST and ALONE (D21) — nothing else has written yet, so the apply cannot clobber another cell's work. STEP 4 — check the size budget: scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs hard-fails at 20480 bytes and AGENTS.md measured 19181 before this change. If the rewrite pushes past it, tighten the new wording rather than raising the threshold.

truths: The retired sentence appears in zero live copies: the template, all four projections, and the merged root AGENTS.md.
The replacement preserves the unverified-execution caveat — it does NOT claim proven Codex hook parity (docs/06-runtime-integration.md:67 still lists discovery as open).
Every count in live prose matches its measured quantity: 8 Codex lifecycle events, 7 Claude lifecycle events, 9 hook scripts. No surviving '6-hook' claim.
AGENTS.md stays under the 20480-byte hard fail, and the reduction (if any was needed) came from tightening wording, never from raising the threshold.
artifacts: The rewritten doctrine sentence, the corrected corners, and all four regenerated projections plus the merged root AGENTS.md
prohibitions: Never edit AGENTS.md's bee block by hand as the primary fix — it is merged from the template and the next render reverts it.
Never raise HARD_FAIL_BYTES or WARN_BYTES in scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs. Clearing a red by widening the threshold is not the same act as fixing what is measured.
Do not touch docs/history/** — captured transcripts and archived reports are not live copies.

trace.dropped_reason: Revision 2 failed validation (6 CRITICAL). Superseded by revision 3 under D23-D26: widened regen trigger, PreCompact row split, four-way count vocabulary, corrected handoffOutcome premise.
